name: latest-version
usage_summary: Used in packages/cli-kit/src/public/node/node-package-manager.ts (line 12 import, line 714 call). The single call site is `getLatestNPMPackageVersion(name)` which wraps `latestVersion(name)` in a timing wrapper. This function is used to check whether a newer version of a CLI package is available (upgrade notifications). The test file mocks it with `vi.mock('latest-version')`.
how_to_remove: Replace the `latestVersion(name)` call with a direct fetch to the npm registry: `fetch('https://registry.npmjs.org/' + name + '/latest').then(r => r.json()).then(d => d.version)`. This is a one-function change in node-package-manager.ts. Remove the `import latestVersion from 'latest-version'` statement and the `"latest-version": "7.0.0"` entry from cli-kit's package.json. Update the test mock to mock the fetch call instead.
replacement_reason: latest-version is a thin wrapper around a single npm registry fetch. Node.js 18+ ships `fetch` as a global built-in, making the dependency entirely redundant. The replacement is a one-liner fetch call.
